title: Carosite
content:

{{Quote|Eat right and live right.|A Carosite philosophy|<ref name="pg2" />}}
The '''Carosites''' were a [[Mammal/Legends|mammalian]], [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] who hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Carosi IV]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. However, when the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Carosi]] began to rapidly expand, they were forced to evacuate their entire population to the outer planet [[Carosi XII]] and rebuild their civilization from scratch.

Carosites were renowned throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for their medical skills. Their medical academies attracted some of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] greatest [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] to study and teach, drawn by their significant advancements in the treatment of mental illnesses and in [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]] technology. Carosites were generally pacifists, although on occasion they flew into berserker rages to protect their patients. Their reverence for life and medical acumen culminated in their famous [[Hospital ship/Legends|hospital ship]], the ''[[Sudden Restoration]]''. Throughout the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it provided free health care to all, without regard to [[Politics/Legends|political]] affiliation.

==Biology and appearance==
Carosites were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]], standing between 1.3 and 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Their heads, on unusually long necks, sported long snouts and small beady [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Carosites were slim in build and had extremely nimble [[Limb/Legends|hands]] due to the peculiar arrangement of their digits; two opposable thumbs flanked three central fingers. Their bodies were covered in short, fine [[Hair/Legends|fur]] that kept them warm, and they often grew long goatees on their chins. Carosites had particularly acute senses.<ref name="ae" />

Carosites could only [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduce]] twice in their lifetimes, on each occasion producing a litter of up to six young. Families were the only social unit in their communities, and often included older relatives unable to live independently. The Carosite lifespan was 120 [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]].<ref name="ae" />

==Society and culture==
===Healing and medicine===
{{Quote|The business is ailing, but may be healed with proper treatment.|An example of how medical terminology influenced all Carosite culture|<ref name="ae" />}}
[[File:Carosite_with_droid.png|thumb|left|upright|Some Carosites upgraded medical droids.]]
Due to their infrequent reproduction, the Carosites developed a great reverence for life; each being's life was regarded as sacred. The attitude influenced their society in numerous ways, most particularly in their strong medical tradition. They made advances in the recognition and treatment of mental illnesses, with their sanitariums known for their emphasis on healing the individual rather than confinement. They also made innovations in the field of [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]] [[Programmer/Legends|programming]]. As such, upgrading medical [[Droid/Legends|droids]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Carosi XII]] cost only a fraction of what it did elsewhere.<ref name="pg2" /> To maintain their medical facilities, the Carosites traded heavily with the [[Ebranite]]s for [[lu-ramin]], an [[Oil/Legends|oil]] vital for several of their medicines.<ref name="gg12">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref> Carosites charged for their healing services commensurate to the patients' ability to pay. One distinctive feature of their care was their refusal to install [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic enhancements]], but they were willing to install prosthetics which did not enhance patients' abilities.<ref name="pg2" />

Carosites enjoyed passing on their medical heritage, so medical academies were established on their planet, such as the [[Carosi XII Academy of Medicine]].<ref name="air">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> These institutions attracted famous [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]], [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]], and surgeons to study or teach. The [[Carosite language]] was influenced by such heritage, with medical metaphors commonly used to describe business deals, galactic politics, and other non-medical areas of life.<ref name="ae" />

Their reverence for life produced a strongly pacifist culture, although an exception was made for the protection of the feeble, the helpless, and their patients. Under such circumstances, Carosites were known to fly into a berserker state, sometimes called a "life-saving fanaticism."<ref name="pg2" /> Furthermore, the Carosites abhorred any form of interrogation or [[Brain/Legends|brainwashing]], regarding private thoughts as inviolate. However, a vocal minority argued for more aggressive action against oppressors such as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. This group called themselves "[[The Preventers]]," and argued that assertive action would save lives in the long run.<ref name="ae" />

The Carosites were aware of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and desired to learn more about it. However, no Carosite was ever trained as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], as their curiosity revolved around using it to promote healing and recovery, rather than for other purposes. Their attitude toward the Force was typical of the innovative species, some of whom even tried to find ways to harness [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology to promote healing.<ref name="pg2" />

===Government===
The Carosites developed a form of democracy whereby every ten years they elected a leader who held absolute authority. This leader relied on eleven counselors for advice, selected from the species' eleven major families.<ref name="ae" /> Carosite society valued truth, and so considered verbal and written agreements completely binding. As a manifestation of their eternal optimism, they extended this expectation to offworlders, who frequently abused such trust and failed to uphold their bargains.<ref name="pg2" />

Carosites disliked [[Alcohol/Legends|alcohol]] due to its harmful effects on sentients, both in the short and the long term. The disdain led their government to tax liquor sales heavily and minimize the number of liquor outlets on their planet.<ref name="ae" /> Their paternalistic attitude led Carosites to frequently offer unsolicited dietary advice. Visitors often regarded such doting as worse than that typical of [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]].<ref name="pg2" />

The sole city on Carosi XII was [[Newlife Point]], which was home to most of the two million Carosites living on the planet. The city was established on the shores of the [[Avuae Sea]], where the original refugee ships had landed. The sea was rich in marine life, which provided a good source of protein, and the Carosites were fond of swimming in the icy [[Water/Legends|waters]]. Just south of this city were a number of hot pools, heated by underground volcanic activity. The hot springs were known to aid in the healing process and so the Carosites linked it to Newlife Point with an underground transrail system.<ref name="pg2" />

==History==
[[File:Carosite.jpg|thumb|right|upright|250px|A Carosite holding a medical tool]]
The Carosite species evolved on the planet Carosi IV in the [[Carosus system]],<ref name="pg2" /> part of the [[Nembus sector/Legends|Nembus sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Their homeworld was located near the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]], a vital [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] through that part of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and in an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that was explored between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5,000]] to [[3000 BBY]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Carosites traveled offworld<ref name="TOR">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=crewskill|Investigation|Medical Emergency}}</ref> as early as [[3638 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{TOR_updates|2.0}}</ref> a period that included the [[Galactic War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|resurgent Sith Empire]].<ref name="TOR" />

In [[200 BBY/Legends|200 BBY]], the [[Star/Legends|sun]] in the Carosites' home-system began an unusually rapid expansion that threatened their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The situation led to a major upheaval in Carosite society, and over the course of twenty years, they evacuated their entire civilization to Carosi XII, an ice planet further out in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The expanding sun consumed the first five planets of the system and soon increased the temperature of Carosi XII to merely "cool," rendering the world more hospitable.<ref name="pg2" /> The Carosites slowly [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] Carosi XII over the next two centuries, making use of scientists and other specialists to rebuild their world.<ref name="ae" />

The Carosites were deeply affected by their turbulent dislocation and struggled to find their bearing in the galaxy for many generations. It caused many to value old traditions and rituals, producing a veneration of the past. However, a small fragment of their society pushed for the forging of new traditions to meet their new circumstances. This caused heated debate among the population.<ref name="pg2" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Carosites were loyal to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], but their respect for life led them to be undiscerning in whom they treated, prompting Carosite physicians to provide medical care for Imperial survivors as much as Rebels. The Carosites had little military strength and maintained only a small navy and merchant fleet.<ref name="pg2" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Alliance made a significant [[Battle of the Carosi system|strike]] against the Empire in the Carosus system.<ref name="Atlas" /> After the Rebel Alliance transitioned into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the Carosite relationship with that government was further strengthened by [[Omo Taj]], the Carosite leader at the [[Time/Legends|time]]. He authorized the construction of a [[New Republic outpost|secret base]] on Carosi XII, and he subsequently maintained good relations with the base [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Balderik Rajana]]. However, the base did draw Imperial attention to Carosi XII. On one such occasion, an [[Unidentified Imperial agent (Carosi XII)|agent]] trained [[rinacat]]s, native predators, to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] the base operatives.<ref name="pg2" />

Later during the New Republic era, the Carosites established [[pleasure dome]]s on Carosi XII, some making use of expensive [[synthdroid]]s, which increased travel to the planet such that the [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] was in constant use.<ref name="swe">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', under the Carosi entry.</ref> The [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]] established a synthdroid factory on the [[moon of Carosi XII]], which was visited by [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]].<ref name="pot">''[[Planet of Twilight]]'' states that the facility was on "Carosi's larger moon". However, Carosi XII was said to have only one moon in ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]'', so perhaps this was on another of the remaining planets in the system.</ref>

==Carosites in the galaxy==
[[File:Tamo_Lan.png|thumb|right|upright|Tamo Lan, Rebel Alliance physician]]
Carosites were most frequently encountered in the galaxy as physicians and doctors, such as the pair that worked as medics at the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]].<ref name="air" /> A notable Carosite physician was [[Tamo Lan]]. Trained in medicine on Carosi XII, Lan joined the Rebel Alliance after killing a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who threatened one of his patients. He served the Alliance on [[Baes Logia]] and the [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Temple]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] before joining [[Shroud Team]] as a medic. When his best friend, the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Karlon]], was [[Battle of Bresnia|killed]] on [[Bresnia]], he took in Karlon's sister, [[Gondara]].<ref name="swaj10">{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)}}</ref> Another esteemed [[Unidentified Carosite doctor|Carosite doctor]] went on a mission during the Galactic War, and disappeared. He resurfaced more than five years later, around 3638 BBY, without any memory of where he had been, and was thought to have been kidnapped.<ref name="TOR" />

Not all Carosites were so selfless; the cyberneticist Dr. [[Parnath]] was a notorious exception. Exiled from Carosi for his unethical practices and relentless pursuit of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], he moved to the [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]] where he performed any cybernetic enhancements requested of him, provided the payment was good.<ref name="sepg">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref>

Even more prominent than these individuals were the Carosite physicians aboard the [[Hospital ship/Legends|hospital ship]] the ''[[Sudden Restoration]]''. A modified [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] [[Class-C frigate/Legends|HS-1 hospital ship]], it traveled the galaxy providing free medical care to all, and acquired a legendary patient recovery rate. It was given its name because it often visited the sites of recent battles, appearing out of nowhere to care for the wounded and civilian populations. The [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] reputation was tarnished somewhat when [[Unidentified Carosite physician|one of its physicians]] was blackmailed by the Empire into serving as their agent. He was putting Republic officers into comatose states and then jettisoning their "[[Death/Legends|dead]]" bodies into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], where the Imperials later picked them up for interrogation.<ref name="pg2" />

The ''Sudden Restoration'' offered medical care to the sick and wounded, regardless of their political affiliations. Because of this liberal approach, it was largely free from attack, with [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] known to hunt down any who dared menace the ship. However, on one occasion the ship was attacked by a [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Sudden Restoration attack)|bounty hunter]]. He had been hired by a [[Unidentified crime lord (Sudden Restoration attack)|crime lord]] to assassinate an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Sudden Restoration patient)|Imperial officer]] who was receiving treatment on the ship. Afterward, the New Republic insisted on stationing [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] aboard to provide small fighter cover to the ship. The ship was so successful that the Carosites considered producing more like it. Many of its patients were later transferred to [[Carosi Alpha Hospital]] on Carosi XII itself.<ref name="pg2" /><ref name="swpc">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref>

[[File:Carosite_physicians.png|thumb|left|Carosite physicians worked aboard the ''[[Sudden Restoration]]''.]]
The ''Sudden Restoration'' was open to different methods of healing, and for a time the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Keoulkeech]] served aboard it. A Carosite [[Transport/Legends|transport]] on its way to [[Yir Tangee/Legends|Yir Tangee]] had encountered the Ewok while on a stopover at [[Salfur's Trading Post]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Recognizing his ability, they had transferred him to the ''Sudden Restoration''.<ref name="hr">''[[Heroes & Rogues]]''</ref>

During the Galactic Civil War, the Empire captured a [[Unidentified Carosite smuggler|Carosite smuggler]]. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] then used the dark side of the Force to burn his mind, transforming him into a feral beast, driven by fear, and used him as a fanatical guard. In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], New Republic agents [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] encountered him on [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]] in the underground catacombs.<ref name="coj">[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]</ref> Carosite beakers were also used by the genetic terrorist [[Zeta Magnus]] during his occupation of the planet [[Skye/Legends|Skye]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Skye">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Carosites first appeared in ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]'', produced by [[West End Games]] in [[1992]]. The species then received mentions in a succession of other sourcebooks produced for WEG's ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Two [[Bantam Spectra]] novels, [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']] and ''[[Planet of Twilight]]'', also referenced the species and their homeworld.

In [[2009]], the Carosi system was placed by ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''; this placement shed light on the context of the species and planet's history. ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]'', an update released in [[2013]], created a Carosite doctor as a possible quest for players to assign one of their companions on individual quests.
